customTools.description: Player rings, tolls, peals, sounds, or otherwise activates the bell.

customTools.$comment: Read an item with readable text. Generic handler: gates on accessibility + readability, surfaces the item's readable.text via the {arg.itemId.readable.text} template. Side effects (puzzle triggers like LLD ritual completion) are wired via separate triggers gated on intentMatched(read) + intentArg(read, itemId, ...).
customTools.description: Read the contents of an item with readable text — book pages, leaflet, prayer inscription, matchbook cover, etc. Pass the id of an item from the current view's itemsHere or inventory.

itemId.description: id of the item to read

customTools.$comment: READ OTHER PAGES of the black book — canonical hint mechanism (per docs/puzzles.txt:51). The book's marked page (569) holds the prayer; other pages mention 'certain noises, lights, and prayers' are effective in banishing evil. Player can flip through anywhere they have the book.
customTools.description: Player flips through, browses, skims, or reads pages other than the dog-eared marked page (569) of the black book. NOT for reading the prayer itself (that's the regular `read` action on the book).

preconditions.failedNarration: You don't have the black book to flip through.

handler.successNarration: You leaf through the heavy black book, past the dog-eared page 569 to other passages. Among the dense archaic script, one line stands out: "In the banishing of evil, certain noises, lights, and prayers prove most effective."

customTools.$comment: BOAT REPAIR with putty (in tube). Active only when boat is punctured and player carries putty. Consumed on use.
customTools.description: Player plugs, repairs, patches, or applies putty to the punctured boat.

customTools.$comment: BOAT LAUNCH: shove the inflated boat off from the dam-base shore into the river. Player must be inside the inflated boat at dam-base. Backed by the boat-launches trigger which moves the boat (and the player riding it) to river-1, where the per-river drift triggers take over. With player-as-item, only the boat moves — the player rides along via container parentage.
customTools.description: Player launches, shoves off, casts off, releases, or pays out the boat into the river.

triggers.$comment: Canonical Zork JIGS-UP: central death handler. Every fatal trigger writes its flavor to flag.death-message and flips just-died=true; this trigger picks up the cascade. Drops carried items at the player's current room (via the <current-room> sentinel), extinguishes lit light sources, increments the deaths counter, and branches on death count: deaths<3 → respawn at forest-1 with a 'voice intervenes' line; deaths>=3 → endGame to the Land of the Living Dead. Clears the just-died flag and death-message at the end so the trigger doesn't loop. Regular (NOT afterAction) trigger so it joins the fixed-point cascade after a fatal afterAction trigger sets just-died — Engine.execute runs a follow-up regular runTriggers pass after the afterAction phase that picks this up.

then.message:
**** You are dead ****

The Land of the Living Dead opens to receive you. As you cross its threshold, the air thickens with the murmur of those who came before — adventurers, fools, the unlucky. You take your place among them.

else.text: From a great distance, an unseen voice intones: "Adventurer, you have not yet earned the deepest dark." A hand you cannot see takes you up.

triggers.$comment: Rope-and-dome puzzle. Player ties the rope to the railing in the dome-room → unblocks the dome-room.down → torch-room exit. Rope moves out of inventory and onto the railing (modeled as 'rope at dome-room'). once: false (paired with untie-rope-on-take) so tie/untie cycles work — the player may descend, climb back up, take the rope, walk around, return, and re-tie. Gates on the generic tie intent + (rope, railing) arg pair. removeMatchedIntent provides per-attempt single-shot: each tie intent fires this trigger exactly once, then clears, ready for the next attempt.
triggers.narration: You tie the rope securely to the railing. It hangs down into the room below.

triggers.$comment: Auto-untie when player retakes the tied rope. After tie-rope sets dome-flag=true and parks the rope at dome-room, the player can climb back up via dome.up from torch-room and `take rope` to retrieve it. Without this trigger, dome-flag would stay true while the rope is back in inventory — leaving the dome.down exit open with nothing actually anchoring it. This trigger watches for rope re-entering inventory while dome-flag is true and clears the flag (re-blocking the descent until the rope is tied again). once: false so the player can tie/untie repeatedly.
triggers.narration: As you take the rope, it slips free of the railing.

triggers.$comment: Songbird opens the egg when the player gives it to her. Gates on the generic give intent + (egg, songbird) arg pair + player at any room where the songbird is perceivable. Consumes the give intent so the give-rejects catchall doesn't fire.
triggers.narration: You hold out the egg. The songbird hops to it and sings a single perfect trill — and the delicate clasp springs open, revealing a golden clockwork canary nestled inside.
You drag the rug aside. Beneath it, set flush into the floor, is a closed wooden trap door.

triggers.$comment: Magic word (Ulysses / Odysseus) — cyclops panics, smashes east wall, flees. Sets cyclops-flag (gone) AND magic-flag (east passage now open to living-room). Also clears combat-engaged-with-cyclops for hygiene; the attacks-player gate already fails because cyclops is at 'nowhere', but stale engagement state should not persist.
triggers.narration: The cyclops's eye widens in stark terror. He bellows, scrambles backward, and crashes east through the wall — leaving a cyclops-shaped hole that opens straight into the living room. The way up the staircase is now unguarded.

triggers.$comment: Granite-wall teleport — North Temple → Treasure Room. Canonical Zork I V-TREASURE (gverbs.zil): saying 'temple' or 'treasure' at the temple moves the player to the thief's treasure room. removeMatchedIntent consumes the word so the paired treasure→temple trigger can't bounce the player straight back in the same cascade.
triggers.narration: The word leaves your lips and the granite wall seems to drink it in. A grey lurch swallows the room — and when it lets go, the wall stands behind you, on its far side.

triggers.$comment: Granite-wall teleport — Treasure Room → North Temple. The return half of the canonical V-TREASURE teleport.
triggers.narration: You speak the word; the granite wall yawns into grey nothing, the world heaves once, and sets you down beyond it.

triggers.$comment: Feed lunch to cyclops — one half of the peaceful resolution. Gates on the generic give intent with targetId=cyclops AND itemId=lunch OR sandwich-bag (canonical Zork is lenient about which the player names). Per-arg accessibility check ensures the trigger only fires when the SPECIFIC item is reachable. Priority 10 fires before give-rejects catchall; cyclops-falls-asleep at priority 5 fires after, in same iteration, when both halves are in.
triggers.narration: The cyclops snatches the sandwich and devours it in two bites. He grunts and licks his lips.

triggers.$comment: Mirror of cyclops-eats-lunch for the drink half. Gates on give intent with targetId=cyclops AND itemId=bottle OR water (canonical Zork allows both phrasings: 'GIVE BOTTLE' or 'OPEN BOTTLE THEN GIVE WATER').
triggers.narration: The cyclops drains the bottle in one long pull, water running down his beard.

triggers.$comment: Sleep the cyclops once both halves of the meal are in. Priority 5 so it fires AFTER the eats/drinks triggers in the same iteration (fedLunch + fedBottle are both true by then). Same final state as cyclops-flees: cyclops-flag set, unconscious, combat cleared. magic-flag intentionally NOT set on the fed path (no wall break — staircase up only, no living-room shortcut east).
triggers.narration: Sated and slaked, the cyclops slumps against the wall and begins snoring thunderously. The staircase is no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: Eat lunch — only edible item in this story. Trigger gates on intentArg=lunch + accessibility (handler refused calls won't fire this; trigger conditions don't run substitute-args, so we enumerate per-item rather than use {fromIntent}). When more edible items get added, add a sibling trigger.
triggers.narration: Thank you very much. It really hit the spot.

triggers.$comment: eat catchall — clears the matched intent if no specific eat-X trigger fired (e.g. handler refused with 'not edible' or with 'don't have it'). Priority -100 fires last. Empty narration so it doesn't add a cue beyond the handler's failedNarration.
Drink water — only potable item in this story. Same per-item shape as eat-lunch.
triggers.narration: Thank you very much. You were rather thirsty.

triggers.$comment: Pour water — only pourable in this story. Per-item gate; fires only when player named water AND water reachable.
triggers.narration: The water spills out and vanishes into the ground.

triggers.$comment: Consume the intent so the player can pray again later from the temple. Without removeMatchedIntent the trigger would re-fire every turn after the first prayer (matchedIntents persists), teleporting the player on every action.
triggers.narration: You kneel before the altar. A gentle warmth gathers around you, and the temple fades. When your senses clear, you are standing in a sunlit forest.

triggers.$comment: TROLL COMBAT — STATUS-AWARE OVERRIDE. Player attacks an unconscious troll for an instant kill, regardless of which weapon. Listed first via priority 100 so it consumes attack-this-turn before any class trigger fires. Demonstrates the pattern: more-specific triggers (status-aware) win over general (class).
triggers.narration: The troll lies helpless. Your weapon descends and ends him with a single, brutal blow.

triggers.$comment: MODE OVERRIDE: throwing the axe at the troll. Listed at priority 50 (above the class trigger) and gated on attack-mode='throw'. Either hits or misses, but the axe always ends up on the floor (player loses the weapon either way — that's what throwing means).

then.text: The axe whirls end-over-end and buries itself in the troll's chest. He topples.
The axe slams into the troll's shoulder, drawing blood. He pulls it free and drops it.

else.text: The axe sails wide and clatters against the wall.

triggers.$comment: SPECIFIC OVERRIDE: rusty knife is feeble vs the troll. Lower kill weight, less damage, more misses than the class trigger. Priority 50 so it wins over the bladed-class trigger (priority 10).

then.text: Your rusty knife scratches the troll.
You stab the troll deep — the knife's tip snaps off.

else.text: The troll sneers and bats your knife aside.

triggers.$comment: CLASS TRIGGER: any bladed weapon vs the troll. Covers sword + axe (swing) + any future bladed weapon — one trigger via flagItemHasTag. Priority 10 (below specific overrides). Self-cleans attack-this-turn so the cleanup catchall is just safety. Refuses if weapon is stuck or troll is dead.

then.text: Your blade bites the troll for a light wound; greenish blood wells.
Your blade carves a serious gash across the troll's chest.
A killing blow! The troll roars and topples in a pool of greenish blood.
Your blade strikes the troll's temple — he crumples, unconscious.
Your blade sweeps the troll's leg — he goes down hard.
Your blade cracks the troll's axe-grip — the bloody axe spins to the floor.
Your weapon sticks fast in the troll's hide — you can't pull it free!

else.text: The troll dodges your swing.

triggers.$comment: TROLL'S SWING AT PLAYER. Tick trigger: fires every turn while combat-engaged-with-troll is set AND the troll can act (alive, not unconscious, not knocked-down). Drains flag('player-health'); player-killed trigger ends the game on zero.

then.text: The troll's axe nicks you.
The troll's axe bites deep!
The troll's axe strikes a mortal blow!

else.text: The troll swings wildly and misses.

triggers.$comment: Troll status recovery: knocked-down troll heaves himself back up next turn. Author chose immediate recovery; an alternative would be a multi-turn counter.
triggers.narration: The troll heaves himself back to his feet.

triggers.$comment: Combat disengages when the player flees the troll-room. Engagement flag clears so combat-troll-attacks-player stops ticking. The troll stays in the room (just no longer attacking the absent player).

Catchall: ensures attack-this-turn never persists across turns even when no specific/class trigger matched (e.g. attack troll with leaflet). Priority -100 so it fires last. Combat triggers self-clean redundantly; this is the safety net.

Stuck-weapon recovery (active): player explicitly tries to free their weapon. 60% success per attempt; takes a turn either way. Per-weapon branches because the engine has no way to ask 'which of these is currently stuck?' as a single Condition — three random weights, one per possible stuck weapon.

then.text: You wrench the weapon free.

else.text: You pull, but the weapon stays fast.

triggers.$comment: Stuck-weapon recovery (passive safety net): each turn while a weapon is stuck, count up; after 3 turns it works loose on its own. Ensures the player isn't permanently disarmed. One trigger per weapon — could be tag-driven later.

triggers.narration: Your sword finally works free with a wet jerk.
Your axe finally works free with a wet jerk.

triggers.$comment: Troll dies when his HP drops. Drops his axe (canonical), opens the east/west exits via troll-flag, removes the body. Clears the engagement flag so combat-troll-attacks-player stops. Regular trigger (cascades from combat tick effects).
triggers.narration: The troll collapses in a pool of greenish blood. His bloody axe falls at his feet. The east and west passages out of the room are no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: Death-narration override: dying in maze-5 (the skeleton room) gets the canonical 'you have joined its inhabitants' flavor. Priority 100 + once:true means it fires before the generic player-killed trigger; it sets death-handled=true to consume the death event so player-killed's gate fails. Same consume-the-flag pattern used by the combat triggers' attack-this-turn cleanup.

effects.value: Your breath rattles, and you slump beside the pile of bones. You have joined its inhabitants.

triggers.$comment: Generic player-death gate. Any trigger that drains player-health below zero ends the game with this catchall message — UNLESS a more specific death trigger (priority > 0, gated on context) consumes the event by setting flag('death-handled')=true. The message is intentionally context-free so it works for cyclops, troll, grue (oh no — actually grue has its own endGame), thief, or any future hostile. The death-message setFlag is wrapped in an `if` so a specific killer (e.g. cyclops-attacks-player) that already wrote a flavored line into death-message wins; the catchall only supplies its generic message when nothing else has.

then.value: Your wounds overcome you, and the world fades to black.

triggers.$comment: Canonical Zork: the first time the player descends into the cellar with the trap door open, an unseen hand (the thief, or some unknown agency) slams it shut from above. once:true so it's a unique one-time event. The player can't reopen it from below (trap-door.openWhen blocks playerAt(cellar)), so they have to loop through the underground (cellar → south → east-of-chasm → gallery → studio → up the chimney → kitchen → west → living-room) to reach the trap door from above again.
triggers.narration: From above, an unseen hand slams the trap door shut. You hear the click of a latch.

triggers.$comment: Lamp-light success path. Generic light handler already set lamp.isLit=true; this trigger emits the canonical narration and consumes the matched intent so it doesn't re-fire within the same cascade. The lamp-light-no-battery trigger (priority -10) handles the dead-battery case by reversing isLit back to false.
triggers.narration: The brass lantern flickers on, casting a warm glow.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: lamp battery is dead. Handler set isLit=true; reverse it and narrate. Priority -10 so this fires after lamp-lights would have (which wouldn't fire because battery==0 fails its gate).
triggers.narration: The lantern's battery is dead. Nothing happens.

triggers.$comment: Lamp-extinguish success path. Generic extinguish handler already set lamp.isLit=false; this trigger emits canonical narration and consumes the intent.
triggers.narration: The lantern goes dark.

triggers.$comment: Tick: while the lamp is lit, drain one battery turn per action. afterAction + once:false fires each turn.
Tick: at zero battery, force-extinguish the lamp. Cascade trigger handled by phase-3 fixed-point. once:true so the canonical narration only fires once.

then.text: Your lamp dims, flickers, and goes dark.

triggers.$comment: Tick: while the candles are lit, drain one burn-turn per action. Mirrors lamp-burns-fuel.

Tick: at zero burn-time, force-extinguish the candles. Mirrors lamp-runs-out, but once:false so the cue plays each time the player relights spent candles (and they immediately re-extinguish). The candles-light-spent soft-fail intercepts that case at the light step instead.

then.text: Your candles have burned down to nothing — the flames sputter and die.

triggers.$comment: Canonical CAVE2-ROOM gust: at the end of any turn the player is in tiny-cave carrying lit candles, a ~50% gust blows them out (Zork I <PROB 50 80>; 50 = normal play). Only isLit is cleared — burnTurnsRemaining is untouched, so the LIGHT MATCH / LIGHT CANDLES WITH MATCH relight path still works. No analog to the ZIL <DISABLE <INT I-CANDLES>> is needed: candles-burn-fuel already gates on isLit, so it stops draining on its own.

then.text: A sudden gust of wind sweeps through the cave and snuffs your candles out.

triggers.$comment: GRUE: ticks the darkness-turns counter while the player is in pitch black. Player dies on the SECOND consecutive dark turn — first turn shows pitch-black warning, second turn the grue gets them. Counter resets when light is restored.

effects.value: Oh, no! You have walked into the slavering fangs of a lurking grue!

triggers.$comment: GLOBAL TURN COUNTER. Reusable infra: increments every action, no gating. Drives the thief's first-appearance threshold and any future time-based puzzles. afterAction:true so it ticks even on rejected actions.

TROLL AGGRAVATION (NPC-INITIATED COMBAT). Every time the LLM matches the talk-to-troll intent, this tick fires. Bumps troll.state.aggravation by 1, consumes the intent so it can fire again next turn, and emits a growl cue. Three matches push him over the edge (see troll-snaps).
triggers.narration: The troll bares his yellow tusks and growls.

triggers.$comment: TROLL SNAPS — threshold trigger. When aggravation hits 3 and he isn't already engaged, flip combat-engaged-with-troll. The existing combat-troll-attacks-player tick takes over from the next turn. Priority 50 wins over passive baseline triggers.
triggers.narration: Your prattling has worn through the last shred of the troll's patience. 'TROLL SMASH!' he bellows, and lunges at you.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS HUNGER TICK. Each turn the player lingers in cyclops-room, his hunger ticks up. Inactive once cyclops-flag is set (he's been appeased — magic word OR fed). Random branches at hunger=3 and hunger=6 add menacing flavor cues; the actual engagement happens via cyclops-snaps below.
TALK-TO-CYCLOPS BUMP. Talking to the cyclops aggravates his hunger faster (+1 per talk on top of the passive tick). Consume intent so it can fire next turn.
triggers.narration: The cyclops eyes you with growing impatience.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS HUNGER MILESTONE — early warning at 3. Once-only, narrates the rising tension. No state change beyond a cue.
triggers.narration: The cyclops sniffs the air greedily and takes a step toward you.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS SNAPS — threshold trigger. Hunger >= 6 boots the cyclops into autonomous combat. Same engagement-flag pattern as the troll. Priority 50 wins over passive baselines.
triggers.narration: The cyclops's patience snaps. 'I HUNGER!' he bellows, and his great hand swings down at you!

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS ATTACKS PLAYER. Tick trigger — fires every turn while engaged + cyclops can act. Cyclops is huge: harder hits than the troll, plus the unique 'lift and throw' / 'grab and crush' outcomes. Drains player-health.

then.text: The cyclops cuffs you with the back of his hand — your vision swims.

then.value: The cyclops's hand catches you across the temple. Your vision swims, then fades.

then.text: The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall!

then.value: The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall — and you do not get up.

then.text: The cyclops grabs you in a crushing grip — you feel ribs crack!

then.value: The cyclops squeezes. Ribs crack. Then the rest of you.

then.text: The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. Stars bloom behind your eyes.

then.value: The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. The stars behind your eyes go out.

else.text: The cyclops swings — too slow. You dance aside.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS BLADED CLASS TRIGGER. Player-initiated combat against the cyclops. He's huge — sword/axe/knife mostly bounce off; rare deep cut, no instant kill, no disarm or stun. Authors voice: cyclops is a tank. Priority 10 (below specific overrides if any are added later).

then.text: Your blade glances off the cyclops's hide — a shallow scratch. He laughs.
Your blade finds a gap in the cyclops's hide and bites deep!

else.text: You swing — the cyclops doesn't even flinch. Your blade rings off his skin.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS DIES. Mirror of troll-dies. Clears engagement, removes the cyclops, sets cyclops-flag (which the magic-word/feed paths also set — they unblock the staircase). Doesn't smash the wall; only the magic-word path does that (it sets magic-flag too).
triggers.narration: The cyclops's massive bulk shudders, then crashes to the floor with a sound like a falling tree. He does not move again. The staircase is no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS DISENGAGE on flee. Mirror of combat-disengage-on-flee for the troll.

THIEF FIRST APPEARS. After 30 global turns, the thief slips into one of the major underground rooms (random pick). Once-only; appeared:true gates further wandering. He starts in round-room per ZIL extraction; this trigger relocates him into the pool of wanderable rooms so he isn't always at the same first-encounter spot.
triggers.narration: Somewhere in the darkness, you hear the soft scuff of a boot on stone. You are no longer alone in the underground.

triggers.$comment: Thief wanders unless he's actively engaged in combat with the player OR the player has invaded the treasure-room (canonical: once the hideaway is violated, the thief stays at treasure-room defending it until killed). The combat-engaged-with-thief gate (added by thief-combat-engages) pins him during a fight; the not(triggerFired(treasure-room-invaded)) gate pins him after the invasion.

THIEF STEALS. When the thief and the player are co-located AND the player was already sized up last turn (canon: leave-immediately escape valve), small chance per turn he lifts a treasure that's actually within reach (inventory OR on the room floor). Each branch's `if` guards on (treasure in inventory) OR (treasure at the same room). The 60% 'nothing' branch has no `if` so it's always eligible — when no treasure is reachable, that branch fills the draw and the thief walks past empty-handed. Skipped while combat is engaged (he's busy fighting, not stealing).

then.text: A black-gloved hand snakes toward your pocket — you slap it away.
'A trifle, surely?' the thief murmurs, and the egg vanishes from your possession.
'How droll.' The bag of coins slips from your grasp into the thief's cloak.
The chalice is plucked from in front of you with a flick of the thief's wrist.
The painting — gone. The thief inclines his head in mock apology.
The jade figurine disappears into the folds of the thief's cloak.
The scarab — gone in a heartbeat. The thief tips an imaginary hat.
'A pleasure.' The bracelet is plucked from your wrist (or the floor) into the thief's palm.

triggers.$comment: THIEF BLADED CLASS TRIGGER. Player-initiated combat with a bladed weapon. Thief is fast and slippery — high miss rate, modest damage when you hit, instant-kill on a critical. Priority 10.

then.text: Your blade lands — a glancing wound. The thief hisses.
Your blade bites deep. The thief reels.
It's curtains for the thief as your nasty knife removes his head.

else.text: The thief evades your blade.

triggers.$comment: THIEF DIES. Drops everything he was carrying back to the room (including stolen treasures). The thief item itself goes 'nowhere'.
triggers.$dropEverythingComment: Bulk-move everything currently in the thief's possession to treasure-room. moveItemsFrom only moves items whose location === 'thief' — fixes the prior bug where this trigger unconditionally teleported jade/scarab/bracelet/etc. from their original rooms even when the thief never stole them. ORDER MATTERS: dump-from-thief BEFORE moving the thief to 'nowhere', otherwise any held items would end up at 'thief'@'nowhere' (unreachable).

effects.$comment: The large-bag is fixed scenery the canonical thief carries; moveItemsFrom would dump it to treasure-room as orphaned scenery whose 'underneath the thief' description leaks the impression that the thief is still present. Send it to 'nowhere' so it vanishes with him in the canonical fog.

triggers.narration: Almost as soon as the thief breathes his last breath, a cloud of sinister black fog envelops him, and when the fog lifts, the carcass has disappeared. As the thief dies, the power of his magic decreases, and his treasures reappear in the Treasure Room.

triggers.$comment: THIEF COMBAT ENGAGES. Priority 50. When the player attacks the thief, flip combat-engaged-with-thief; the wander trigger is gated false on this flag, so the thief stays put for the duration of the duel. Mirrors troll-snaps. The flag clears when the thief dies (extended in thief-dies) or when the player flees the room (thief-combat-disengages-on-flee).
triggers.narration: The thief draws his stiletto with a sigh of regret — 'A duel, then. How... regrettable.'

triggers.$comment: Thief disengages when the player leaves the room he's in (canonical 'leave to escape'). Engagement flag clears so the thief is free to wander again, and the next time the player meets him the encounter restarts with sizing-up. Symmetric to combat-disengage-on-flee for the troll.

THIEF SWINGS AT PLAYER. Tick trigger: every turn while combat-engaged-with-thief is set AND the thief can act, drains player-health. Canonically the thief is much more dangerous than the troll — heavier weights on the high-damage and instakill branches. The generic player-killed (or any priority>0 thief-specific death narration) ends the game once player-health hits zero.

then.text: The thief lunges; you barely evade. The stiletto comes within a hair of you.
The stiletto bites home; the wound is shallow but it bleeds freely.
The blade slips past your guard and drives deep. You stagger.

else.text: A flick of the wrist, faster than you can follow — the stiletto plunges deep.

triggers.$comment: Thief-specific death narration. Priority 100 (above the generic player-killed at priority 0). Consumes the death-handled flag, ends the game with thief-flavored prose. Canon flavor: the thief regrets the unpleasantness, but a duel is a duel.

effects.value: The thief, a man of culture, regrets the unpleasantness — but a duel is a duel. He wipes the stiletto on his cloak and is gone.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — player wounded (band: 12 < hp ≤ 20). Per-turn while ANY combat is engaged. No memoization; fires every afterAction tick within the band. The narrator STYLE rule about varying repeated combat cues handles per-turn variation.

effects.text: Your breath catches; the wounds are starting to tell.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — player bloodied (band: 5 < hp ≤ 12).

effects.text: Blood slicks your hands and slows your grip.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — player critical (band: 0 < hp ≤ 5).

effects.text: The world narrows. Each step costs more than the last.

triggers.$comment: PASSIVE HEALING — adapts canonical I-CURE (1actions.zil:3593-3602): while wounded, the player recovers over time. afterAction tick: +1 player-health each turn the player is below max (30) and NOT engaged in combat. Silent — canon healing prints nothing; DIAGNOSE reports it. The <30 gate self-limits at max, so +1 never overshoots.

DIAGNOSE report (canonical V-DIAGNOSE, 1actions.zil:3993-4024). Fires on the diagnose intent; narrates the player's wound state from player-health bands, plus a death count. removeMatchedIntent (the give-rejects pattern) so this once:false trigger doesn't re-fire in the fixed-point cascade — its narrate-only effects make no state change otherwise.

then.text: You are in perfect health.
You have a light wound — scrapes and bruises, nothing more. It will mend on its own as you rest. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)
You have a serious wound. Your strength will return if you rest and keep out of danger. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)
You have serious wounds and should not press your luck. Rest, away from danger, and they will slowly mend. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)

else.text: You are barely alive. One more solid blow would surely finish you. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)

then.text: You have been killed before; the Great Underground Empire does not grant many second chances.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — troll wounded (band: 4 < hp ≤ 6 of max 10). Per-turn while combat-engaged-with-troll.

effects.text: The troll's roars grow ragged; he favors one leg.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — troll bloodied (band: 2 < hp ≤ 4).

effects.text: The troll snarls, blood matting his fur; his axe arm trembles.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — troll critical (band: 0 < hp ≤ 2).

effects.text: The troll sways, axe drooping; one more blow looks likely to finish him.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — cyclops wounded (band: 10 < hp ≤ 16 of max 25).

effects.text: The cyclops bellows in pain, his single eye wild.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — cyclops bloodied (band: 4 < hp ≤ 10).

effects.text: The cyclops's breath comes in wet rasps; blood streaks his chest.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — cyclops critical (band: 0 < hp ≤ 4).

effects.text: The cyclops staggers, breath labored, the bloodlust dimming.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — thief wounded (band: 12 < hp ≤ 20 of max 30).

effects.text: The thief's elegant guard frays; sweat darkens his cloak.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — thief bloodied (band: 5 < hp ≤ 12).

effects.text: The thief's smile is gone; he fights now with grim economy.

triggers.$comment: TIER CUE — thief critical (band: 0 < hp ≤ 5).

effects.text: The thief's stiletto wavers; his composure cracks at last.

triggers.$comment: THIEF SIZES UP. First turn the player and the thief share a room (and combat hasn't begun), the thief studies the player without attacking — canonical 'leave immediately and you're safe' escape valve. Sets thief-sized-up-this-encounter; the steal trigger only fires after this is true (i.e. on the SECOND turn of co-location). Priority 20 to fire BEFORE thief-steals (which has implicit priority 0). Resets when the player leaves (thief-encounter-resets).
triggers.narration: The thief looks you over with cool calculation, weighing what you carry. He does not attack — yet.

triggers.$comment: TREASURE-ROOM FIRST-ENTRY. Canonical Zork: 'You hear a scream of anguish as you violate the robber's hideaway.' The thief teleports into treasure-room (if not already there); his bag stays closed but now also contains the silver chalice (moved from the room into his bag, joining whatever he's previously stolen). After the player kills the thief, dump-from-thief moves everything from the bag into treasure-room — chalice + stolen items reappear. once:true — fires exactly once. Priority 50 so it runs before thief-sizes-up (priority 20) and thief-steals (priority 0) on the climb-up turn, ensuring the canonical sequence: invasion → sizing-up → eventual combat.
triggers.narration: You hear a scream of anguish as you violate the robber's hideaway. Using passages unknown to you, he rushes to its defense. The thief gestures mysteriously, and the treasures in the room suddenly vanish.

triggers.$comment: Thief-encounter reset. Once the player and thief are no longer co-located, clear thief-sized-up-this-encounter so the next meeting starts fresh from sizing-up. Symmetric to thief-sizes-up; runs whether or not combat was engaged.

Give the closed jewel-encrusted egg to the thief — he opens it cleanly and pockets both the egg and the canary inside. Second canonical egg-opening path (give-egg-to-songbird is the first). Gates on the generic give intent with itemId=egg + targetId=thief, plus itemAccessible(thief) — replaces the per-room enumeration of (player+thief in same room) with a single accessibility check. once:true; the egg + canary return to the treasure-room when the thief dies (handled by thief-dies trigger). removeMatchedIntent(give) so the give-rejects catchall doesn't also fire.
triggers.$canaryComment: Canary's location stays at 'egg' — container parentage is transitive in the engine, so when the open egg moves into the thief's pocket the canary travels with it; when thief-dies later moves the egg back to treasure-room, the canary follows. The egg arrives open, so take(canary) traverses the open container.
triggers.narration: The thief takes the egg between two long fingers, examines it, and gives a small approving smile. With an audible click the clasp yields. He pockets the egg, canary and all. 'A pleasure, as always.'

triggers.$comment: Thief auto-opens any closed egg he ends up with — covers the case where he STOLE the egg from the player rather than receiving it via give. Canonical Zork: 'The thief will take the egg to his lair and open it.' once:true. priority:10 so it fires BEFORE thief-dies (priority 0), ensuring the egg is opened before the dump-from-thief moves it out of the bag — otherwise the egg would dump still-closed when the thief dies the same turn. The give path is handled by thief-opens-egg (which sets isOpen=true and consumes the intent); the not(triggerFired) clause + the isOpen=false gate together ensure this doesn't double-fire. Empty narration — the egg-opening happens offscreen in his closed bag.

Give the thief ANY item — canonical ROBBER-FUNCTION (1actions.zil:1986-2012): GIVE <anything> to the thief and he pockets it in his great bag. priority -50 so the closed-egg special case (thief-opens-egg, default priority 0) wins first, and the give-rejects catchall (-100) loses. removeMatchedIntent so the catchall doesn't also fire. Items the thief holds drop to treasure-room when he is killed (thief-dies), so stashing treasures with him is non-destructive. The given item's accessibility is already guaranteed by the give customTool's handler precondition — trigger when-conditions don't run {fromIntent} substitution, so it isn't (and need not be) re-checked here.
triggers.narration: The thief accepts your offering, turns it over in his long fingers with a connoisseur's eye, and tucks it away in his great bag with a small, satisfied nod.

triggers.$comment: give catchall — consumes the matched intent for any (item, target) pair without a specific trigger. Priority -100 so it fires after every per-pair give-X-to-Y trigger. Generic narration; the LLM can re-flavor based on the visible target.
triggers.narration: There is no reaction.

triggers.$comment: Unlock the grate with the skeleton keys. Per-target trigger for the unlock customTool. Sets passageState.grate.isLocked=false and consumes the intent so the unlock-rejects catchall doesn't fire.
triggers.narration: The skeleton key fits the skull-and-crossbones lock with a satisfying click; the grate's bolt slides free.

triggers.$comment: Lock the grate with the skeleton keys. Mirrors grate-unlocks. Refuses if grate is currently open (canonical: must close before locking).
triggers.narration: You turn the skeleton key in the skull-and-crossbones lock; the bolt slides home.

triggers.$comment: Refusal when player tries to OPEN the grate while still locked. The generic open handler runs FIRST and sets isOpen=true (it doesn't know about isLocked); this trigger reverts isOpen=false and emits the canonical 'skull-and-crossbones' refusal so the LLM gets the colorful prose, not just the bland '{name} is locked' default.
triggers.narration: The grate is locked closed — a skull-and-crossbones lock keeps it shut.

triggers.$comment: Unlock catchall — fires when no per-target trigger consumed the intent. Generic refusal so the LLM doesn't fall through to invented prose.
triggers.narration: The key doesn't fit.

triggers.$comment: Lock catchall — symmetric to unlock-rejects.
triggers.narration: The key doesn't fit.

triggers.$comment: Player explicitly unties the rope (separate from untie-rope-on-take, which fires when the player takes the rope back). Gates on untie intent + rope-at-dome-room (i.e. currently tied). Reverses tie-rope: clears dome-flag, returns rope to inventory.
triggers.narration: You untie the rope from the railing and coil it back into your hand.

triggers.$comment: Tie catchall — fires when no per-target trigger consumed the intent. Generic refusal so the LLM doesn't fall through to invented prose.
triggers.narration: There's nothing to tie that to here.

triggers.$comment: Untie catchall — symmetric to tie-rejects.
triggers.narration: There's nothing here to untie.

triggers.$comment: Talk-to catchall — fires when no per-target trigger consumed the intent.
triggers.narration: There is no reaction.

triggers.$comment: Burn catchall.
triggers.narration: It won't catch fire.

triggers.$comment: Mung catchall.
triggers.narration: Nothing happens.

triggers.$comment: Push catchall.
triggers.narration: Nothing happens.

triggers.$comment: Lower catchall.
triggers.narration: There's nothing to lower here.

triggers.$comment: Raise catchall.
triggers.narration: There's nothing to raise here.

triggers.$comment: Turn catchall.
triggers.narration: It doesn't turn.

triggers.$comment: Inflate catchall.
triggers.narration: There's nothing to inflate here.

triggers.$comment: Plug catchall.
triggers.narration: Nothing to plug.

triggers.$comment: Dig catchall.
triggers.narration: Nothing to dig here.

triggers.$comment: light catchall — fires when no per-item light trigger consumed the intent (lamp/match/candles handle their own; the always-lit torch and anything else fall through here). Generic refusal so the LLM doesn't narrate a phantom success.
triggers.narration: It is already alight.

triggers.$comment: extinguish catchall — fires when no per-item extinguish trigger consumed the intent.
triggers.narration: There is nothing here to put out.

triggers.$comment: move-the-rug catchall — fires when not in living-room, or the rug has already been moved.

then.text: The rug already lies in a heap to one side.

else.text: There is no rug here to move.

triggers.$comment: cyclops-magic-word catchall — fires when the word is uttered anywhere the cyclops-flees trigger doesn't pick it up.

then.text: Your word rings out, but the cyclops is already long gone.

else.text: You utter the word, but nothing here stirs to it.

triggers.$comment: say-granite-word catchall — fires when 'temple'/'treasure' is spoken away from a granite wall.
triggers.narration: You speak the word aloud. Nothing answers.

triggers.$comment: pray-at-altar catchall — fires when the player prays away from the altar.
triggers.narration: You bow your head, but this is no place of worship.

triggers.$comment: free-stuck-weapon catchall — fires when no weapon is currently stuck.
triggers.narration: Nothing of yours is stuck fast.

triggers.$comment: say-echo-in-loud-room catchall — fires when 'echo' is said outside the loud-room, or after it has already been solved.

then.text: Your voice carries clear through the quiet room.

else.text: Your voice goes out and simply fades.

triggers.$comment: wave-scepter-at-rainbow catchall — fires without the sceptre, away from the rainbow, or after it is already solidified.

then.text: You raise the sceptre and wave it, but nothing here answers the gesture.

else.text: You have no sceptre to wave.

triggers.$comment: wind-canary catchall — fires when the canary isn't to hand.
triggers.narration: You have nothing like that to wind.

triggers.$comment: brush-teeth-with-putty catchall — fires when the player has no putty.
triggers.narration: You have nothing to brush them with.

triggers.$comment: leap-down catchall — fires when there is nothing here to leap down from.
triggers.narration: There is nowhere here to leap down to.

triggers.$comment: attack-self catchall — fires when the player carries no weapon.
triggers.narration: You have nothing on you to do that with.

triggers.$comment: swim-river catchall — fires when there is no river here to swim in.
triggers.narration: There is no water here to swim in.

triggers.$comment: rub-mirror catchall — fires when there is no mirror here.
triggers.narration: There is no mirror here to rub.

triggers.$comment: repair-boat-with-putty catchall — fires when the boat isn't punctured or the player has no putty.

then.text: You have the putty ready, but nothing here is torn or leaking.

else.text: You have nothing to patch anything with.

triggers.$comment: Force-break the egg (smash / crush / break) — destroys the canary inside. Mirror of egg-falls-from-tree but for explicit break-verb attempts in any room; the canonical Zork I 'sickening crunching noise' outcome. Both items get state.broken=true; egg.isOpen flips to true (broken == open for take semantics). The thief-opens-egg gate prevents retroactively destroying a thief-extracted canary. Variants on the egg/canary items render the post-break description. once:false is explicit — engine's default for once is true, so we must override. Idempotency comes from the not(broken=true) gate plus removeMatchedIntent(break) in the effects, both of which prevent re-fire after the first successful run; relying on those instead of once:true means a stale firedTriggers entry from any prior buggy run can't lock the trigger out forever.
triggers.narration: There is a sickening crunching noise. The egg's clasp gives way, and the bird inside is silenced under the wreckage.

triggers.$comment: Player force-opens the egg directly — canonical Zork I 'indelicate handling' outcome: opens AND damages both egg and canary inside. Layered after the open handler's 'You open the jewel-encrusted egg.' success cue. Gate clauses: (1) intentMatched(open) + (2) intentArg target=egg + (3) not(broken=true) for undefined-tolerance + (4) not triggerFired(thief-opens-egg) so the trigger only fires on the player's force path, not after the thief has already cleanly extracted the canary. once:false is explicit — engine's default for once is true, so we must override. Idempotency comes from the not(broken=true) gate plus removeMatchedIntent(open) in the effects; relying on those instead of once:true means a stale firedTriggers entry can't lock the trigger out forever.
triggers.narration: Your indelicate handling bends the egg's clasp out of true and buckles the gold inlay. The clockwork bird inside is mangled — silver beak crumpled, ruby eyes scratched, fine wires kinked beyond use.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #2 COFFIN AT ALTAR — canonical: SOUTH-TEMPLE-FCN sets COFFIN-CURE = NOT(coffin in inventory) every turn at south-temple. We mirror with two symmetric triggers — one to set the flag when player arrives without coffin, one to clear it if they re-pick up the coffin. The down→tiny-cave exit unblocks/re-blocks dynamically.
triggers.narration: A cool draft seems to flow up from the floor of the temple — a passage somewhere has opened.
As you take the coffin in hand, the draft from below ceases. The way down has sealed itself again.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #6 ECHO ROOM — canonical: bare 'echo' verb in loud-room's special command loop sets LOUD-FLAG and clears SACREDBIT on the platinum bar. We use visibleWhen on the bar (already added in items overrides) so the bar materializes once echo-spoken is true. Removes the matched intent so it can fire on later visits if needed (though it's gated on echo-spoken=false so it's effectively once).
triggers.narration: Your voice goes out — and instead of the cacophony you've been suffering, perfect silence answers. The acoustics of the room have changed. A platinum bar gleams on the floor where the noise had hidden it.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #3 WAVE SCEPTRE AT RAINBOW — canonical: SCEPTRE-FUNCTION sets RAINBOW-FLAG and clears INVISIBLE on POT-OF-GOLD. We use visibleWhen on pot-of-gold (already added in items overrides). Skip the canonical toggle-off-on-second-wave; once solid, stays solid.

then.text: You raise the sceptre and trace a slow figure in the air. The rainbow shudders and solidifies — its colors hardening into a luminous bridge of gold and prismatic light. At your feet, a heavy pot of gold appears, gleaming where the rainbow now touches earth.

else.text: You raise the sceptre and trace a slow figure in the air. The rainbow shudders and solidifies — its colors hardening into a luminous bridge of gold and prismatic light, arcing solid across the falls.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #4 ENDGAME — when the win condition is met, set won-flag (which unblocks west-of-house→stone-barrow). Narrates the canonical 'almost inaudible voice' line. once:true. Kept as a regular trigger so it fires on the same turn the win condition becomes true (cascading through the fixed-point loop). Uses itemContainedBy (not itemAt) so nested treasures count — canary inside egg-in-case, sceptre inside coffin-in-case both satisfy the gate.
triggers.narration: An almost inaudible voice whispers in your ear: 'Look to your treasures for the final secret.' Across the room, the trophy case glitters; in the depths of the wood, an ancient map appears — and far above ground, the way to a stone barrow stands open.

triggers.$comment: Stone-barrow finale — canonical STONE-BARROW-FCN runs FINISH (game over with credits). We end the game when the player enters.

effects.message:
**** You have completed the Great Underground Empire ****

A voice calls out from the depths of the barrow: 'Welcome, adventurer. Your final score: {flag.score} (out of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This earns you the rank of {rank}. Your name shall be inscribed in the chronicles of those who have braved the Empire and prevailed.'

The stone slab settles back into place behind you. Your adventure is at an end.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #5 MIRROR ROOM — RUB the mirror to teleport between mirror-room-1 and mirror-room-2. (Canonical also swaps room contents to maintain the 'same room' illusion; we skip that — the LLM can narrate the disorientation.)
triggers.narration: You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.
You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #8 BAT CARRY — when the player is in bat-room without garlic, the vampire bat seizes them and drops them at a random underground location (canonical FLY-ME picks from BAT-DROPS). Gated on bat-carried-this-visit so the trigger fires once per visit, not every fixed-point iteration. Reset trigger below clears the flag when player leaves the room.

then.text: Fweep! The bat seizes you by the scruff of the neck and lifts you away through utter blackness. You are deposited, dazed, in a coal mine.
Fweep! The bat carries you through tunnels you cannot see, depositing you in another part of the mine.
Fweep! The bat hauls you off into the darkness and drops you somewhere deep underground.
Fweep! The bat seizes you and bears you off to a distant shaft.
Fweep! The bat carries you up a narrow shaft and drops you at the top.
Fweep! The bat plummets with you down a long, dark drop and releases you at the bottom.
Fweep! The bat takes you on a winding journey and drops you in a squeaky-floored cavern.

else.text: Fweep! The bat carries you back near where the mining began.

triggers.$comment: Reset bat-carried-this-visit when player is no longer in bat-room, so the bat will grab them again on the next visit (canonical Zork lets the bat re-grab on every entry).

PUZZLE #1 BELL + BOOK + CANDLES — canonical Zork I LLD ritual. Single bell-rings trigger handles every ring of the bell, anywhere. Outer if/then on hasItem(bell). Inner if/then on playerAt(entrance-to-hades): hades branch advances the puzzle (sets bell.state.rangAtHades, drops bell, drops the candles if held — extinguishing them if still lit; gated on holding, not lit, since a wind gust may already have snuffed them en route); elsewhere branch is flavor narration only. Effects are idempotent so re-rings produce the same end state with the same canonical narration. Heat is purely narrative.

then.text: In your confusion, the candles drop to the ground (and they are out).

The bell suddenly becomes red hot and falls to the ground. The wraiths, as if paralyzed, stop their jeering and slowly turn to face you. On their ashen faces, the expression of a long-forgotten terror takes shape.

else.text: The bell tolls — a deep, hollow note that hangs in the air for a long moment, then fades.
There's no bell in your hand.

triggers.$comment: Capstone of the LLD ritual — read the book at entrance-to-hades while bell.rangAtHades=true and candles are lit. Sets lld-flag, opening the in/south exits to land-of-living-dead. The bell does NOT need to be in inventory at this step (canonical: it lies on the floor where it fell after ringing).
triggers.narration: Each word of the prayer reverberates through the hall in a deafening confusion. As the last word fades, a voice, loud and commanding, speaks: "Begone, fiends!" A heart-stopping scream fills the cavern, and the spirits, sensing a greater power, flee through the walls.

triggers.$comment: Match strike success path. Generic light handler set match.isLit=true; this trigger does the canonical side effects: decrement matchesRemaining, start the 2-turn burn-countdown, narrate. Gates additionally on matchesRemaining > 0 so the match-strike-empty soft-fail (priority -10) handles the empty-matchbook case.
triggers.narration: One of the matches starts to burn.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: matchbook is empty. Reverse the handler's isLit=true mutation and narrate. Priority -10 so it fires after any same-priority success path would have.
triggers.narration: The matchbook is empty — you've burned all your matches.

triggers.$comment: Candles light success path. Requires a currently-burning match (canonical: must strike a match first) AND remaining burn-time. Handler set candles.isLit=true; this trigger consumes the match (sets isLit=false) and emits canonical narration. The burnTurnsRemaining > 0 gate prevents the player from wasting a match on spent candles — candles-light-spent (priority -20) handles that case.
triggers.narration: You touch the burning match to the candles, lighting them.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: tried to light candles without a burning match. Reverse the handler's isLit=true mutation and narrate. Priority -10.
triggers.narration: You have nothing burning to light them with.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: candles are spent (burnTurnsRemaining<=0). Reverse the handler's isLit=true mutation and narrate. Priority -20 (lower than candles-light-no-match) so we only fire when there IS a burning match — otherwise the no-match path narrates first.
triggers.narration: The candles are spent — only stubs of wax remain. There's nothing left to light.

triggers.$comment: Candles extinguish success path. Generic extinguish handler set candles.isLit=false; this trigger emits canonical narration.
triggers.narration: You blow out the candles.

triggers.$comment: Torch can't be extinguished (canonical: torch is perpetually lit, wreathed in flame). Generic extinguish handler set torch.isLit=false; this trigger reverses the mutation immediately and narrates the refusal. Functions as a soft-fail no-op.
triggers.narration: The flames cling to the torch — it can't be extinguished.

triggers.$comment: Cower cue: when the player lights the candles at entrance-to-hades AFTER bell rang at hades. Once-per-game dramatic narration matching canonical text. priority 50 ensures it fires BEFORE candles-light (priority 0) removes the matched intent in the same cascade iteration. Gated on match.isLit=true (same as candles-light) so a no-match attempt — which the light tool flips isLit=true on transiently before candles-light-no-match reverses it — does NOT consume this once:true narration.
triggers.narration: The flames flicker wildly and appear to dance. The earth beneath your feet trembles, and your legs nearly buckle beneath you. The spirits cower at your unearthly power.

triggers.$comment: Tick the match-burn countdown each turn while a match is burning. Mirror of low-tide-countdown / bell-cooldown patterns.
When the burn countdown hits 0 and the match is still burning, extinguish it. Canonical 'The match has gone out.' — player must strike a fresh match to retry.

then.text: The match has gone out.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: read the prayer at hades without bell.rangAtHades. State description, no engine state change.
triggers.narration: You read the prayer aloud. The words have no power; the spirits jeer.

triggers.$comment: Soft-fail: read the prayer at hades with bell rung but candles unlit. State description, no engine state change.
triggers.narration: You read the prayer aloud. The words fade in the dim light, leaving no impression.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #7 COAL → DIAMOND MACHINE — canonical: TURN switch WITH screwdriver only fires the conversion if machine lid is CLOSED. We gate the intent on machine being closed; this trigger additionally checks coal is inside the machine. On success: coal → nowhere, diamond → machine-room (player can then take it).
triggers.narration: The machine comes to life with a dazzling display of colored lights and bizarre noises — clattering, whirring, sparks. After a few moments the racket subsides. The lid pops open. Where you placed the coal, a magnificent diamond now lies.

triggers.$comment: Failure case: TURN switch with non-coal in machine (or empty). Canonical produces 'gunk'. We just narrate frustration to keep the puzzle solvable without polluting the inventory.
triggers.narration: The machine clatters and whirs, but produces only a faint puff of greenish smoke. Whatever it's looking for, it isn't finding.

triggers.$comment: Failure case: TURN switch with screwdriver, but the machine lid is OPEN. Distinct narration from no-coal (which is lid-closed): smoke escapes the unsealed chamber, the run aborts. Hint-free.
triggers.narration: The machine clatters into life. A plume of green smoke curls up out of the chamber, drifts into the room, and dissipates. The noise dwindles to silence.

triggers.$comment: Catches turn-machine-switch with anything OTHER than the screwdriver named (lid-open and no-coal cases are handled by their dedicated triggers above). Lower priority so the dedicated triggers win when applicable.
triggers.narration: The switch doesn't budge.

triggers.$comment: Bare-hand attempt at the machine switch. Fires on push-machine-switch (no-args customTool) — the LLM picks this when the player says 'push start' with no tool named. Canonical refusal: switch is too small for fingers.
triggers.narration: The switch is too small to manipulate with human fingers — barely 1/16 by 1/4 of an inch.

triggers.$comment: BASKET LOWERS. Triggered by the lower-basket intent at either shaft-room or lower-shaft, when the basket is currently raised. Moves the basket to lower-shaft and flips its position state. Items inside the basket follow it transitively (the engine traverses item-inside-item parentage). The intent is consumed so it can fire again on the next round-trip.

effects.value: lowered

triggers.narration: The chain rattles as the basket descends out of sight, settling at the bottom of the shaft.

triggers.$comment: BASKET RAISES. Symmetric to basket-lowers. Triggered by raise-basket intent from either shaft-room or lower-shaft when the basket is lowered. Returns the basket (and contents) to shaft-room.

effects.value: raised

triggers.narration: The chain creaks as you haul; the basket rises smoothly out of the depths and settles at the top of the shaft.

triggers.$comment: Enter-room cue for lower-shaft (Drafty Room). Fires after `go` lands the player here, gated on basket position. Uses the new built-in intent recording (intentMatched(go) + playerAt) to detect room entry. Surfaces the canonical 'basket overhead / at your feet' narration as a state-change CUE rather than relying on the basket's appearance variant — cues are more reliably woven by the LLM than view-embedded text. Consumes the go intent so the trigger doesn't ghost-fire on later in-room actions.

then.text: Far above you at the top of the shaft, the wicker basket sways gently on its chain — well out of reach.
The wicker basket sits at your feet at the bottom of the shaft, the chain rising into darkness above.

triggers.$comment: Symmetric enter-room cue for shaft-room. When player enters with basket lowered (chain dangles), surface the absence-cue. Default raised case needs no cue — room description + default basket appearance already cover it.

then.text: The basket has been lowered out of sight; the chain disappears down into the shaft.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #9 DAM CONTROL PANEL — multi-step. YELLOW button enables bolt control (gate-flag). BROWN button disables it. With gate-flag set, TURN BOLT WITH WRENCH at dam-room toggles gates-open. Opening gates queues an 8-turn delay (low-tide-countdown). When countdown hits 0, low-tide flag flips → reservoir exits open.
triggers.narration: Click.
Click.

triggers.$comment: RED button — cosmetic only. Toggles the maintenance-lights flag (no engine effect — maintenance-room is already lit per canon). The LLM gets a tool to call when the player pushes red, instead of standing there confused.

then.text: Click. The maintenance-room lights flicker out. After a moment they flicker back on — a self-resetting safety circuit, evidently.

else.text: Click. The maintenance-room lights flicker.

triggers.$comment: BLUE button — starts the leak-and-flood puzzle. leak-active flips to true, leak-flood-counter starts at 5 turns. Per canonical Zork, water rises over several turns until the player either drowns OR plugs the leak with the viscous material (putty). Once active, the leak runs every turn regardless of player location.
triggers.narration: There is a rumbling sound and a stream of water appears to burst from the east wall of the room (apparently, a leak has occurred in a pipe). The water level begins to rise.

triggers.$comment: Leak flood tick. Decrements leak-flood-counter each turn while the leak is active and the player is at maintenance-room (canonical: water only rises while you're standing in it — leaving the room buys you time, but doesn't stop the leak).
Leak flood drowning death. When counter hits 0 with leak still active and player still at maintenance-room → endGame(won=false).

effects.value: The water reaches the ceiling and drowns you in the small space. You should have plugged that leak.

triggers.$comment: Plug the leak with putty. Consumes the putty (moveItem to nowhere — viscous material is now in the pipe), clears leak-active and the counter, narrates success.
triggers.narration: You press the viscous material against the pipe; it spreads and seals the leak with a satisfying squelch. The flow stops. The water level slowly recedes.

triggers.$comment: Bare-hand attempt at the leak. Hint-free thematic refusal — describes failure without pointing at the solution.
triggers.narration: Water sprays past your fingers, the pressure forcing your hand right back.

triggers.$comment: Bolt toggles the gates open (when currently closed). Starts the 8-turn drain countdown. Gates on intentArg withItemId='wrench' so the player must explicitly name the wrench, not just have it in inventory.
triggers.narration: The bolt turns slowly. Somewhere below, the sluice gates open and water pours through the dam with a sustained roar. The reservoir will take some time to drain.

triggers.$comment: Bolt toggles the gates closed (when currently open). Cancels the drain. Same intentArg withItemId='wrench' gate as bolt-opens-gates.

effects.$comment: DO NOT reset low-tide on close. Per canonical Zork (puzzles.txt:43-47), the loud-room becomes 'eerily silent' for a brief window after closing gates BECAUSE the reservoir is drained and water no longer flows through OR over the dam. low-tide persists until the reservoir-refill counter expires (reservoir-refills trigger).

triggers.narration: The bolt turns smoothly under the wrench. Below, the sluice gates rumble shut, and water begins to back up behind the dam once again.

triggers.$comment: Bolt-turn fallback. Fires at dam-room when turn-dam-bolt is matched but neither bolt-opens-gates nor bolt-closes-gates fired (wrong tool, missing wrench, or no gate-flag). Always consumes the intent so it doesn't carry across turns. The playerAt(dam-room) gate is also load-bearing for tool surfacing — without it, the relevance check makes turn-dam-bolt a dynamic intent in EVERY room.
triggers.narration: The bolt doesn't budge — you'll need the right tool, in the right place, with the right control surface enabled.

triggers.$comment: Bare-hand attempt at the dam bolt. Fires on turn-dam-bolt-by-hand (no-args customTool) — the LLM picks this when the player says 'turn bolt' / 'twist bolt' with no tool named. Canonical refusal: bolt is heavy iron and held tight; bare hands won't budge it.
triggers.narration: The bolt is heavy iron and held tight in its socket. Your bare hands can't budge it.

triggers.$comment: Tick the drain countdown each turn while gates are open and countdown > 0. Mirrors the lamp battery / grue tick patterns.

When countdown hits 0 and gates still open, flip low-tide. Silent state change — no narration. The reveal happens via room/item variants when the player visits an affected location (reservoir, reservoir-south, reservoir-north, loud-room, dam item). Narrating here would leak visual detail to players who can't see the reservoir from where they are.

Reservoir refill tick. Once gates are closed and we're in the silent window (low-tide=true AND gates-open=false), counts down each turn. When the counter hits 0 the next trigger flips low-tide back to false (reservoir has refilled and water flows over the dam top again). Mirrors low-tide-tick on the open path.

Reservoir refilled — water level rises back above the dam top, water cascades over again. low-tide → false. The loud-room becomes loud once more. Closes the canonical silent window the player exploits to take the platinum bar. Silent state change — no narration. Reveal happens via room/item variants on the player's next visit (mirrors low-tide-fires; same line-of-sight reasoning).

PUZZLE #10 (bonus) EMPTY-HANDED COAL MINE — canonical: timber-room ↔ lower-shaft is a narrow squeeze that requires the player to carry NOTHING. Tracks inventoryCount in real time via two symmetric triggers; flag flips automatically on take/drop. The flag-gated exits (timber-room.west and lower-shaft's two return exits) open/close as a side effect. Player must drop the lit lamp before squeezing through; the lamp glows on the floor of the room they leave. The conditional narrate effects emit a one-shot squeeze-cue at the moment of transition (false → true) ONLY when the player is standing at one of the two relevant rooms — gives the player a directional hint without spoiling the mechanic, and stays silent everywhere else.

then.text: With your hands free, you think you might just be able to squeeze through the narrow passage to the west.
With your hands free, you think you might just be able to squeeze back through the narrow passage.

triggers.$comment: Thematic refusal when the player tries to go west out of timber-room while loaded. Hooks the built-in `go` action via the new intent-recording mechanism — gates on intentArg(go, direction=west) + playerAt(timber-room) + empty-handed=false. Consumes the matched intent so it doesn't re-fire next turn. The exit's `hidden: true` keeps the toggle invisible to the LLM; this trigger supplies the canonical refusal narration on attempt.
triggers.narration: Your load is too great. The passage west narrows to a slit barely a person's width.

triggers.$comment: Canonical maze-9 down warning. The action runs (move resolved) before triggers fire, so we gate on the destination (maze-11) + the intent direction (down). The only way to reach maze-11 via `go down` is from maze-9, so this gate is unambiguous. afterAction:true so it runs single-pass (no fixed-point loop) — without that the cue would re-fire dozens of times per descent because it doesn't change any state. Repeats on every descent (matches ZIL room behavior).

effects.text: You won't be able to get back up to the tunnel you are going through when it gets to the next room.

triggers.$comment: Symmetric refusal for the return trip: lower-shaft → out/east back to timber-room. Same pattern as timber-room-too-loaded but covers both directions players might use to leave the lower shaft.
triggers.narration: Your load is too great. The passage back narrows to a slit barely a person's width.

triggers.$comment: BOAT INFLATE — pump turns the deflated boat into a seaworthy vehicle. Strict screwdriver pattern: the player must literally name the pump (intentArg withItemId == 'pump'). The vehicle.enterableWhen + container.accessibleWhen on the item gate board()ing and content perception on inflation automatically. The tan label lives inside the boat from game-start; container.accessibleWhen reveals it on inflation, so no moveItem is needed.

effects.value: inflated

triggers.narration: The boat hisses and swells into a sturdy yellow raft, ready for the river.

triggers.$comment: Catches inflate-boat with any tool OTHER than the pump named. Lower priority so boat-inflates wins when withItemId == 'pump' AND the pump is accessible. Mirror of machine-switch-wrong-tool / dig-sand-wrong-tool.
triggers.narration: That isn't going to inflate anything.

triggers.$comment: Bare-mouth attempt at inflating the boat. Hint-free thematic refusal.
triggers.narration: You blow until your head spins. The folded plastic doesn't budge.

triggers.$comment: BOAT REPAIR — putty closes the puncture. Returns boat to deflated state (must be re-inflated). Putty consumed.

effects.value: deflated

triggers.narration: You smear the putty over the gash. With a final hiss the rip seals; the boat is patched, deflated, and ready to inflate again. The tube of putty is now empty.

triggers.$comment: BOAT LAUNCHES from dam-base. Fires when the player hits the launch-boat intent while in the inflated boat at dam-base. Moves the boat to river-1. Player rides along via container parentage. The river-1 tick + advance triggers take over from there. Sibling triggers boat-launches-from-sandy-beach and boat-launches-from-white-cliffs-north handle re-launching from those landings.
triggers.narration: You shove off. The current catches the boat and carries you out onto the Frigid River.

triggers.$comment: BOAT RE-LAUNCHES from sandy-beach. Fires after the player has landed on the east shore (river-4 → sandy-beach via go east) and chooses to head back onto the river. Moves the boat to river-4 — the same segment they landed from — so the river-tick triggers immediately resume drifting downstream.
triggers.narration: You push off the sand. The current takes hold and the boat slips back into the river.

triggers.$comment: BOAT RE-LAUNCHES from white-cliffs-north. Fires after the player has landed on the west shore (river-3 → white-cliffs-north via go west / land) and chooses to head back onto the river. Moves the boat to river-3.
triggers.narration: You shove off the narrow strip of beach. The river takes the boat downstream.

triggers.$comment: Launch-boat catchall. The boat-launches-* triggers consume the launch-boat intent only when the player is aboard the inflated boat at a launch point (dam-base / sandy-beach / white-cliffs-north). If none fired, the intent is still here — refuse instead of leaving a silent no-op the LLM narrates as a phantom launch. priority -100 (like unlock-rejects / lock-rejects) so the real per-location launches go first.

then.text: The boat won't set off from here — there's no current to carry it away.

else.text: You push at the magic boat, but it only rocks where it sits. Shoving it from outside accomplishes nothing — a boat carries you downstream only when you are aboard it.

triggers.$comment: WEAPON-IN-VEHICLE PUNCTURES BOAT — sharp object vs thin plastic. Branches by location: on the river (river-1..river-5) the puncture is fatal as the icy current takes the player. Anywhere else (dam-base before launch, white-cliffs beach), the boat collapses and the player is ejected to dam-base. Tag-driven so any new weapon participates.

effects.value: punctured

then.value: Something sharp pierces the boat. The plastic shrieks and the river takes you — icy, fast, irresistible. You are pulled under and do not surface.

else.text: Something sharp slips — the boat hisses, sputters, and collapses to a useless pile of plastic. You're spat out onto the riverbank at the foot of the dam.

triggers.$comment: RIVER CURRENT TICKS — increment a counter each turn the player is in-boat at any river-N. The advance triggers (next block) gate on the counter reaching 2 (gives player one full turn at each river to choose where to land before the current carries them). Five separate triggers per river so afterAction:once-each prevents cascading all the way down in one turn.

RIVER CURRENT ADVANCE — when the per-river counter hits 2, advance player + boat one room downstream and reset counter. River-5 doesn't have an advance — it has the waterfall-death trigger instead. Threshold of 2 means player gets the arrival turn + 1 more turn to land before drifting on.
triggers.narration: The current carries you downstream.
The current carries you downstream, around a bend.
The current carries you faster — the rumble ahead grows louder.
The current sweeps you forward. The roar of falling water fills your ears.

triggers.$comment: RIVER-5 WATERFALL DEATH — at river-5 with counter >= 2, player goes over Aragain Falls. Threshold same as the others (2 ticks of grace). Player has one full turn at river-5 to escape east → shore.

effects.value: Unfortunately, the magic boat doesn't provide any protection from the rocks and boulders one meets at the bottom of waterfalls. Including this one. Aragain Falls roars its indifference.

triggers.$comment: F2 — Gas-room ignites if the player carries an open flame. Lamp is electric and safe; torch + candles are open flames. Match-lit lifecycle is unwired so we don't check it.

effects.value: Oh dear. You seem to have ignited the coal gas. It's great for blowing up dimwitted adventurers who wander into a coal mine with an open flame.

triggers.$comment: F6a — Egg dropped from up-a-tree shatters and breaks the canary inside. Priority 10 — fires before items-fall-from-tree (priority 0) so the broken-state effects run while the drop intent is still live; the canary spills out of the cracked shell to the path below. once:false; idempotency from not(broken=true). Mutex with thief-opens-egg per the open/break triggers.
triggers.narration: The egg hits the path with a sharp crack. The shell splits apart, exposing the broken clockwork canary tangled in the wreckage.

triggers.$comment: Items dropped at up-a-tree fall through the branches to the forest path below. Generic — works for ANY item the player drops here (egg, lamp, garlic, weapons, anything in inventory). Uses the {fromIntent} IdRef to resolve which item was dropped from state.matchedIntentArgs.drop.itemId. Priority 0 (default) — fires AFTER egg-breaks-on-tree-drop (priority 10) so the egg's broken-state effects run first. removeMatchedIntent(drop) consumes the intent so the trigger doesn't re-fire on subsequent ticks.
triggers.narration: It falls through the branches to the forest path below.

triggers.$comment: P7 — WIND CANARY in any forest room → songbird drops the brass bauble. Canonical CANARY-OBJECT routine: requires intact canary in inventory and a forest room (forest-1, forest-2, forest-3, path, up-a-tree). At up-a-tree the bauble lands at path (falls through the branches). once:true makes this fire exactly once across the game, matching the ZIL SING-SONG flag.
triggers.narration: The canary chirps, slightly off-key, an aria from a forgotten opera. From out of the greenery flies a lovely songbird. It perches on a limb just over your head and opens its beak to sing. As it does so a beautiful brass bauble drops from its mouth, bounces off the top of your head, and lands glimmering in the grass. As the canary winds down, the songbird flies away.

triggers.$comment: P7 fallback — wind-canary in conditions that don't trigger the songbird: broken canary (grinding noise), already-sung canary (chirps blithely), wind in a non-forest room (chirps in vain). Lower priority than the success trigger; the success trigger has once:true so after firing it stops matching and this catchall takes over for re-winds. Always consumes the intent.

then.text: There is an unpleasant grinding noise from inside the canary.
The canary chirps blithely, but the songbird does not return.

else.text: The canary chirps, but its song carries little weight here.

triggers.$comment: P7 scoring — bauble find. Awarded once when the bauble first enters the player's inventory. Canonical Zork I: 1 point.
triggers.narration: (You note the brass bauble's worth — +1 point. Your score is now {flag.score}.)

triggers.$comment: P7 scoring — bauble deposit. Awarded each time the bauble enters the trophy case (toggle via bauble-deposited flag). Canonical Zork I dungeon.zil: BAUBLE TVALUE=1.
triggers.narration: The brass bauble joins the trophy case's collection. (+1 point; your score is now {flag.score}.)

triggers.$comment: P7 scoring — bauble debit. Reverses the credit if the player removes the bauble from the trophy case.
triggers.narration: (The brass bauble leaves the trophy case. -1 point; your score is now {flag.score}.)

triggers.$comment: F24 — Brush teeth with putty (the rubbery wad from the toothpaste tube). Canonical: glues mouth shut, respiratory failure. ZIL: 1actions.zil:53. Requires the putty in inventory.

effects.value: Well, you seem to have been brushing your teeth with some sort of glue. As a result, your mouth gets glued together (with your nose), and you die of respiratory failure.

triggers.$comment: F25 — Burn leaves while carrying them. Canonical: 'The leaves burn, and so do you.' ZIL: 1actions.zil:796. Player must have leaves AND a flame source (lit torch, lit candles, or lit match).

effects.value: The leaves burn, and so do you.

triggers.$comment: F26 — Attack rusty-knife as the weapon. Canonical: the knife is possessed; turns on the wielder. ZIL: 1actions.zil:920. Triggered on the attack action's transient flag — fires before any combat-class trigger because of the high priority.

effects.value: As the knife approaches its victim, your mind is submerged by an overmastering will. Slowly, your hand turns, until the rusty blade is an inch from your neck. The knife seems to sing as it savagely slits your throat.

triggers.$comment: F27 — Leap from up-a-tree. Canonical: you fall from the branches and break your neck. ZIL: 1actions.zil:2915.

effects.value: It's a long way down. You hit hard.

triggers.$comment: F28 — Leap from canyon-view (or any high lookout). Canonical: 'It's a shame the leap you just attempted has done you in.' ZIL: 1dungeon.zil:2410.

effects.value: It's a shame the leap you just attempted has done you in.

triggers.$comment: F29 — Burn the black book (in Hades / land-of-living-dead). Canonical: the guardian appears and reduces you to dust. ZIL: 1actions.zil:2203. Requires the book in inventory and a flame.

effects.value: At the moment the flame touches the book, a guardian materializes from the shadows. With a single, terrible gesture he reduces you to a fine ash.

triggers.$comment: F30 — Mung the bodies in entrance-to-hades. Canonical: the guardian places your head on a sharp pole. ZIL: 1actions.zil:2182.

effects.value: A guardian materializes from the gloom. He shakes his head sadly, then places your own head on a sharp pole at the entrance to the temple.

triggers.$comment: F31 — Suicide via the attack-self intent. Canonical: 'If you insist...Poof.' ZIL: gverbs.zil:239. The engine's attack(weapon, target) action rejects target='player' because the synthesized player isn't in story.items (itemById can't find it) — see TASKS.md for the engine follow-up that would let attack(weapon, 'player') succeed. For now we use a customTool intent.

effects.value: If you insist...Poof, you're dead!

triggers.$comment: F32 — Throw an object at yourself. Canonical: breaks your neck falling backward. ZIL: gverbs.zil:1451. Engine has no throw action; we model via a customTool intent that fires the death directly.

effects.value: The momentum of your throw carries you backward; you crack your skull on the ground. Quite an achievement.

triggers.$comment: F33 — Dig on the beach. Counter-based death: each dig increments a counter; the 4th dig collapses the hole and smothers the player. Canonical: ZIL 1actions.zil:2861. Gates on dig-sand-with-tool + intentArg shovel — the screwdriver-style tool-naming pattern.
triggers.narration: You dig deeper into the loose sand. The hole grows.

triggers.$comment: F33 — On the 4th dig, the hole collapses fatally.

effects.value: The walls of the hole collapse around you, smothering you in cold, wet sand.

triggers.$comment: Sandy-cave dig stage 1: first dig. Counter-driven 4-step puzzle (canonical Zork: dig 4 times to uncover the scarab). Each stage trigger fires only when cave-dig-count matches its expected value, narrates the canonical line, and increments the counter. Gates on dig-sand-with-tool + intentArg shovel (screwdriver-style explicit tool naming).
triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
You seem to be digging a hole here.

triggers.$comment: Sandy-cave dig stage 2: hole getting deeper.
triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
The hole is getting deeper, but that's about it.

triggers.$comment: Sandy-cave dig stage 3: walls of sand close in.
triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
You are surrounded by a wall of sand on all sides.

triggers.$comment: Sandy-cave dig stage 4: scarab uncovered. Flips scarab-uncovered=true so the scarab item passes its visibleWhen gate; engine view starts surfacing it from this turn forward.
triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
You can see a scarab here in the sand.

triggers.$comment: Sandy-cave dig past stage 4: scarab already uncovered, hole is as deep as it'll go. Prevents bottomless dig-spam after reveal.
triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
The hole is as deep as it'll go without bringing the cave down on your head.

triggers.$comment: Catches dig-sand-with-tool with anything OTHER than the shovel named. Lower priority so the per-stage and beach triggers win when the shovel + location match. Mirror of machine-switch-wrong-tool.
triggers.narration: That isn't going to dig much sand.

triggers.$comment: Bare-verb dig refusal (any sandy room, regardless of inventory). Mirror of machine-switch-bare-hands. Player has to literally name the shovel to advance the puzzle.
triggers.narration: Digging with your bare hands won't get you very far.

triggers.$comment: F34 — Mung the painting. Canonical: TVALUE → 0 (treasure becomes worthless). We mark painting.broken=true; the score-credit-painting trigger gates on not-broken so it never awards points after this. The trophy-case branch requires playerAt(living-room) so the customTool relevance check doesn't surface 'mung-painting' in every dungeon room once the painting is deposited.
triggers.narration: You shred the canvas into ribbons. Whatever it depicted, it depicts no longer — the painting is now worthless.

triggers.$comment: F35 — Mung the sceptre at the rainbow. Canonical: rainbow collapses, player left hanging in vapor — fatal. ZIL: 1actions.zil:2614.

effects.value: The sceptre snaps in your hands. With it, the rainbow that bridged the falls fades and dissolves; you hang for a moment in empty mist before plummeting to the rocks below.

triggers.$comment: F36 — Swim/jump into the river. Canonical: drowned by the currents. ZIL: 1actions.zil:2673.

effects.value: The Frigid River lives up to its name. The currents catch you and drag you under within seconds; you drown before you can struggle to the surface.

triggers.$comment: Sword glow per canonical Zork I I-SWORD daemon (1actions.zil:3849). Fires every turn the player is carrying the sword and a hostile is in range — emits the bright cue if a hostile shares the room, else the faint cue if one is in any directly-adjacent room. Silent when no hostile is in range. afterAction so it ticks once per turn after action settles. Item.variants on the sword still drive the examine response with the matching tier prose; this trigger surfaces the glow cue passively each turn.

then.text: Your sword is glowing very brightly.
Your sword is glowing with a faint blue glow.

triggers.$comment: Loud Room tick. Approximates the canonical LOUD-ROOM-FCN M-END eject (1actions.zil:1660) using a single afterAction trigger with an if/then/else over loud-room-ticks. Turn 1 (ticks=0): bump to 1 + emit warning. Turn 2+ (ticks>=1): pick one of the canonical LOUD-RUNS destinations (damp-cave / round-room / deep-canyon) via setFlagRandom + nested if, eject, reset ticks to 0 so a re-entry restarts the cycle. The single-trigger design avoids the cross-trigger race where a warning-set flag would be visible to a sibling eject trigger within the same afterAction pass. Gated on NOT (echo-spoken OR (low-tide AND gates-closed)) — the canonical silent state. Both paths (easter-egg echo AND dam-controls drain+close) silence the room.

then.text: The roar is unbearable — your head is already pounding. You won't last another moment in here unless you can silence the noise.

else.text: It is unbearably loud here, with an ear-splitting roar seeming to come from all around you. There is a pounding in your head which won't stop. With a tremendous effort, you scramble out of the room.

triggers.$comment: Loud Room tick reset. If the player walks out of loud-room before being ejected (e.g., go west to round-room manually), reset loud-room-ticks so re-entry starts fresh with a warning instead of immediate eject. Without this, ticks=1 would persist across the leave-and-return and the next entry would eject on turn 1.
